text:
September 4th: Part 18 (94⑱, Kugatsu Yokka (Jū Hachi)) is the 119 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

As Kurapika continues to look down on the two from his airship, Hisoka tells Chrollo that he only pretended to join the Phantom Troupe, suddenly revealing that he faked his Spider tattoo with Texture Surprise as he removes it and tosses it to the side. He assures Chrollo that he doesn't have to hold back since it isn't considered Troupe in-fighting.

With Pakunoda watching them from inside the airship, Chrollo begins to chuckle and assures Hisoka that he wouldn't want to fight him anymore since Kurapika used his Judgment Chain on him and made him unable to use Nen. Left dumbfounded by the revelation, Hisoka returns to the airship with Pakunoda as the two leave Chrollo by himself on the large rock formation.

Pakunoda asks him how he was able to sneak out of the hideout, and Hisoka replies that he has a friend good at disguising himself. As he texts Illumi to tell him that he can leave, he assures her that Chrollo is no longer in any danger from him since he isn't interested in broken toys.

As the two airships head in different directions, Leorio is surprised that Hisoka left without fighting Chrollo, and Kurapika guesses that since Hisoka isn't actually a Troupe member, Chrollo told him about the Judgment Chain and that he couldn’t use Nen. With Leorio left stunned that Hisoka isn't a Spider, Gon and Killua join them as Kurapika quickly apologizes for putting their lives at risk. Gon says that it's fine and asks if he was able to use his chain on Chrollo. Although Kurapika confirms this, he adds that it isn't the end of everything.

Pakunoda calls the Troupe and assures them that Chrollo has been released, promising to explain the details when she returns to the hideout. Once their airship lands at Lingon Airport, Hisoka reveals to her that his real fortune had said he would fight Chrollo on Tuesday and the Troupe would lose half its limbs by the time he left. He says that every action they take changes their fate, saying goodbye as he turns to leave. Walking back to the hideout in the rain, Pakunoda spots a small cat in an alley and playfully meows back at it.

Once she returns and stands in front of the Troupe members with her gun at the ready, she tells Phinks that Chrollo can't come back there. Phinks angrily asks her if she's joking and orders her to explain, threatening to use violence if they don't like her answer. Pakunoda readies her aura and gun, telling herself that she can fire six shots at once, which is the same number of founding members. She asks Feitan, Phinks, Machi, Nobunaga, Shalnark, and Franklin if they trust her as she aims her gun toward them.

As Pakunoda prepares to use her Memory Bomb ability and shoot out all her memories, Phinks worries that she is being manipulated, but Nobunaga gets his attention and tells him that it's really her. After Pakunoda fires one shot into each of their heads, Kurapika's Judgment Chain activates around her heart and kills her as she falls to the ground and asks that she be the last Troupe member to die. With the members left stunned, all of Pakunoda's memories suddenly flood into their minds. Shizuku checks on Pakunoda and confirms that she's dead, asking the others how it happened. Phinks says that he will explain everything and begins to tell the others why Pakunoda made her decision. Now alone, Chrollo looks toward the east and prepares to fulfill the final part of his fortune.
